sentences.text: Hey, Lauren, can you hear me okay?
Yeah, sure can.
How are you today?
Good, how are you?
I'm doing alright, doing alright.
We've got some really rainy weather over here, so try not to let that bum me out on a Wednesday.
Oh no, that's a bummer.
Yeah.
Are we waiting for Seneca today?
I forwarded him the invite, do you know if he…
I've got no response according to my calendar.
Okay, let me send him a quick message and see if he can join us today.
I know he was out all last week.
Oh, maybe.
I am, like, catching up.
Yeah.
feeling.
Let's see what he says.
Remind me where you're at again?
New York.
Okay.
That's right.
Yeah, all that furniture I got, that I was telling you, the summer furniture, it's getting soaked, because I didn't get the covers for it.
I said, oh, it'll be fine.
Yep, yeah, I know, it's like, if you don't have an awning… I mean, even if you do have an awning, you're still gonna get rain on it, but…
Yeah.
You kinda… Yeah.
I think in the summer, it's fine, right?
Because then the next day, you get a 95-degree day, and it…
It drives.
Yeah.
But now that it's kind of cooling down, I imagine everything's just gonna stay damp.
I've never had to worry about these things, so it's, like, kind of fun to learn about all these things I have to be paying attention to.
Yeah, yeah, I don't… I mean, I guess it depends.
I mean, you have humidity there where we don't in Colorado, so…
Right.
I don't know, hopefully it dries, because if not, it probably will get moldy, and you don't want.
Yeah, no, that'll be gross.
Then you'll be looking at new furniture again next year.
All over again.
Yep, yep.
Looks like… Hi, Seneca, thanks for joining.
Hey, Lauren, how are you?
Sorry about that.
No worries, I know you were out last week, so I wasn't sure if you'd be able to join this week.
Yeah.
Well, Seneca, I just wanted to invite you to this call.
We do this… is it every 6 weeks, I think, now?
Yes, every 6 weeks, yeah.
Okay, so we do this call with ApartmentIQ, just kind of a quick touch base, on, you know, how things are going.
things we need help with, assistance, whatever.
We kind of just do a, you know, a 6-week touch base.
So I figured you would be a good person to start including on this, since you are handling primarily all of the…

user, like, access and stuff, so… and also, since I don't have access to the ticketing system, figured, you know, you also know, kind of, if there's repeat issues or anything like that, so… So that's kind of why I invited you to join this call.
Thank you, thank you.
Yeah, so, I guess, Phillip, do you have anything for us first?
Yeah, no, great to meet you, Seneca.
Only thing I wanted to touch base on was the Smartsheet, just a line on how we feel about the process.
I know it's early days, I know we've only had a few come in, but see if there's any feedback there, if we want to tweak it at all.
That's kind of the big thing on my mind right now.
Yeah, I think, you know, I mean, it's very, very early on on the Smartsheet, so.
I almost started a Smartsheet where the new communities will put their information on the Smartsheet, and it's on, like, the new community checklist.
I'll share my.
For when… for when they go live.
So, we made it, like, super basic.
So, this link is now embedded in that, like, the new community, you know, kind of checklist.
Okay.
For that.

So… now what we're doing is they have a link there after they've created it, so this is only for… I think I have it set up, like, a week or… maybe even a month prior, of when their expected, like, go-live date is, so… Okay.
So now… so this is kind of brand new on their side, so we've only added a few, and most of them I've just added.
And then that's now… you are getting an email to… What's the email attached to this, Philip?
When the status goes green.
The email alert goes out.
ServiceNow, right?
Ye.
IT user admin.
Yep, I was gonna say, it's the one Carly gave us.
So yeah, so it goes there.
So then now, you all are getting notified when our communities are going live.
Correct.
For… Correct.
aware, just so you're aware of, like, you know, user updates, kind of things like that.
So, because I know at that point, then tickets will come in on access and things like that, so…
That's the main pain point.
thinking.
That you wanted to solve for.
Yeah.
Yeah, it has been working, and… Terrific.
I love it.
Perfect.
Because it gives me a heads up before anything is happening, and I basically go ahead and provision the users based on Workday in that community, so I go ahead and provision the users at that time.
Love it.
So is this cutting down on the ticket system now?
It will.
Okay.
We just gotta get the, teams acclimated to… you do not have to put in a ticket for ApartmentIQ.
Right.
Right now, what's happening is we're getting this ticket, but we're also getting the CM sending us a ticket, or, you know, or the REM sending a ticket, hey, can you please give me access to this?
And I'm like, okay, well, technically, you know, you already have access, because I got the ticket from ApartmentIQ.
So it makes it easy for me to just… it's like a blanket response, I just take… and take the response that I put on the Apartment IQ ticket.
Yeah.
take it and send it on those two tickets, but it's because the Apartment IQ ticket comes on a schedule.
Like you guys said.
And their tickets come… Randomly.
So I may have already assigned it on Monday, and then I may receive a ticket on Friday.
Hey, can you please give me access to apartment… and I'm thinking to myself, like, hey, you've had it for the last 5 days, you know?
So…
Let me… that's a good point.

Let me check on the wording that we have in the Smartsheet, because there's a chance we haven't updated that verbiage to that they no longer have to put in a ticket, because I think we were kind of wanting to make sure this was running smooth before we eliminated that, so… Let me double-check with Katie and her team, just to make sure the verbiage is correct, that they don't have to put in that ticket anymore with you.
Okay, perfect.
And also to learn, when the ticket does come in, it comes in as a blank.

So now, you know, since you are, you know, the owner of the ApartmentIQ, what I'll do is… when I get the Carbon IQ ticket, I'll put it on your name, so that way, when I close it out, you'll get a response.
Okay.
Okay.
That works great.
And then… I know, we've had issues with, Phil can attest to this, but our asset managers, they.
I was gonna bring up John.
Yeah, John Harkey's kind of the, you know, the most vocal one.
But how… are you… is there a way that you can also automatically, when you're getting these tickets, assign it to the asset managers, too, or do we just still need them to put in a ticket?
For me, on my side?
Yeah.
It's a little bit more difficult with the asset managers, because…
Right.
They…
all over.

Yeah, they have so many different communities, so it would be better if I got a ticket for the asset manager, because with that, I need their name, and I need to know the communities that they need to be assigned to.
Okay.

You know, unless we just make it across the board, asset managers just have access to all properties, and then that way, I can communicate to my team, hey, when you, you know, when you provision the users, just please go and attach all of the asset management team to it.
Yeah, I know we… I know for… at one point we decided against that, because… there were some asset managers that were like, I don't need.
You know, hmm.

if I'm West Coast, but… I mean, there's… yeah, there's just not an easy way to always update it, because even… even the grouping that I know you've kind of mentioned doesn't automatically update, does it, Philip?
Well, if the user is in the West Coast group.
then someone will still need to manage any new West Coast properties and add them to the West Coast group.
That's the remaining manual piece.
But as long as users aren't constantly, oh, I'm West Coast, oh, I'm Central now, oh, I'm actually going to the Southwest, as long as that's not needing to change.
It should consistently update for them, so long as someone is adding new markets into the appropriate region groups.
So we haven't set up that yet, just because there's been so many other changes.
It's kind of like we need to do, like, one thing at a time.
Sure.

So, a suggestion for the asset managers, I know you guys work off of the… I know you guys have a spreadsheet out there somewhere on the operations side where it shows the asset manager that's assigned to the community.
Okay, if you have that, if you share that with me, then what I can do is share it with my team, and we can use that as a source to assign the asset manager when a property goes live.
Okay.
That would make it a little easier for, you know.
All of us, and we won't, you know… have to, you know, really worry about if they have the access, because also the asset managers, have research access as well, right?
Right, exactly.
Yep.

Yeah, so I think if we… if we can get our hands on that, and, you know, and I know you… it's something that you guys send out, so if y'all add us to the distribution list, then we always have a source, and I can, you know, communicate to the team and say, hey, you know, when we have a change from Apartment IQ, we want to make sure that we're using Workday as the place of record.
For the users and associates, and then we use this spreadsheet for the, you know, the item of record for the asset management assignment.

Okay, that's a good point, because if you're automatically getting this, I mean, because that's, like, I want… I want it to be the easiest on you and your team, too, so if we already have… if you already have access to that, which we'll give you, then that almost eliminates… the kind of, like, the stress of, oh my gosh, I need this, why don't I have it yet?
They went live, you know, 30 minutes ago, and I don't have this, because I know that's…
Absolutely.
Absolutely.
Absolutely.
I know, like, John's a big one of that.
It's like, we're already live, and it's like, we've been live for, like, 10 minutes, so…
You know, we all have fun with John in our world, because definitely he's the same with me.
He's like, hey… Seneca, I don't have this, and I'm like, oh, let me go take a look.
You're like, it just came in.
So, yeah.
Okay, I think that sounds like a plan, as far as…
I have one better.
You don't even have to do anything.
I have the production schedule.
I get it every month.
Oh.
And the production schedule tells me the asset management, representative.
Okay.
So.
I can use this, and my team is used to using this, so you don't even have to add me to that.
I will communicate to them and let them know to…
I don't get that production schedule, so that's perfect.
If you have that, then yeah, let's just start using that.
Thank you for that, Lauren, your brother.
Kind of pre-assign them.
You'll love it, it has the community directory on there, and it tells you all the community manager, regional manager, VP, all that good stuff.
Well, I have that… I have the community directory, and I have, like, the regional deal time.
It's like, I have all these things, like… random things, so…
Yeah.
Perfect, yeah, if your team's already used to that, then let's use that, and just make sure we communicate.
We're gonna… when those tickets come through from ApartmentIQ, we'll go ahead and assign the team plus asset management to it.
Okay.
Alright.
I think that's easy, and I hope that cuts down on, kind of, some of these, you know, urgency and… Kind of, you know, user stuff, so…
Yeah.
Because I think that's been our biggest thing, is just kind of familiarizing the team with… with users, and where to go, and all of that, so…
Then there's a little bit of excitement.
I think they get a little excited when they hear it's coming, and they put the ticket in, and it's like, you're not set up yet, you know?
So that's one of the other things that we experience, you know?
Not high volume, but it's still a little bit of volume when it comes to that that they requested, and then.
I'm not quite…
set up yet, you know?
So, it's just those two challenges, you know?
And other than that, it's not excessive, and this new process, I love it.
When I saw the ticket, I was like, oh my god, I love this.
I wish we had it on some of our other applications.
Yeah, well, that's kind of the point, is we hope it does, and…
I mean, we'll probably have some hiccups, because it is new.
Yeah.
But that's kind of the goal, is to hopefully kind of make things easier on… on everyone.
movie.
And then… there was something else I was gonna say, but now I just forgot it, so… I'm not sure.
But… it'll come to me after this call.

Well, it's huge… I'm so glad that it's working out for you guys, because it's a huge help on us, particularly tracking for those units that come in, making sure we're billing units when relevant and when appropriate.
So for instance, Lauren, I'm gonna keep using the go-live request date if we build it early and it's available.
fantastic, I just see that as a cherry on top if your team wants to get in their access early, but we won't bill until that live game.
Okay, perfect.
Oh yeah, that was the thing I was gonna tell Seneca.
I'm not sure if… I think you know, but the… our go-live date for ApartmentIQ is the day that they start pre-leasing, because that's when ApartmentIQ can start pulling prices from the website.
So… I'm not sure if you know that, but just, if you do get random tickets like that, is we can't pull… we can't pull data until we have prices, ultimately, because it's all based on real time.
And it's once they announce releasing for the community, right?
It's, like, once, like, the website, like, turns on with, like, pricing and availability and, like, all of that.
So, like, as soon as… yeah.
Like, so, I mean, if we have… if we've announced, like, pre-leasing will start in a week, we can't really do anything until those prices go live, so… Okay.
Okay, so… so it's best to say, because I'm gonna, I'm gonna follow up with an email to the team and just let them know.
So it's best to say, pretty much.
ApartmentIQ will go live after pre-leasing, you know, we should look forward to that right after pre-leasing.
Yep, exactly, yeah, okay.
Yep.

Like, right during the same time, I mean, we're trying to get everything to be pretty much, like, you know, same day, is once those prices are turned on on our website, then ApartmentIQ can start pulling the data in, and then we can kind of, you know, everything kind of starts at the same time, ultimately.
That's the goal.
Okay.
Perfect.
Yeah.
And so, if people are wondering, like, why they don't have access, or why they can't get access prior to, it's because there's… there's no information, there's no data to pull from, so… Gotcha.
Like, I mean, because the research is great, but the research is just… how would you describe that, Phillip?
Like, the research one is more of just, like.
I mean, it's, like… Yeah, we… I think on-demand kind of reporting is kind of the way we like to say it.
Unowned assets, mostly.
Even hypothetical non-built assets.
So that… the research catch-all is really helpful there, because it can be for just.
Looking into a new region, or a new market.
Right, in comparison to once it goes live, it's actually looking at the real comps.
Yep, exactly.
Community, yeah.
Like, the real, the 100%, like, the live.
Right, right, right.
Not the perspective of what's coming ahead, or what's really not account for the community.
That's where all that falls into the research part of it.
I'm with you.
I was out there in that world for 10 years before I decided to come into the corporate world.
You got out.
Exactly, yep.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So… okay, perfect!

Well, Seneca, I'm really glad you joined, because I think these were some amazing ideas that you were able to share today, and I'm excited for our next call to make sure that things are working smoothly, so…
Yeah, absolutely.
And also, too, just curious, with, Apartment IQ, just recently, we went live with Meet a Lease, and they have an S… They have an SSO function.
that, is now working with Entrata to… Basically, we set them up when we set them up, they initially log in.
What the, meet a lease does is it matches their title and their properties.
to what Entrada is, they talk to each other, and it updates it.

So, I don't know if this is something that, you know, apartment IQ in the future, or whatever the case may be, but it's an amazing feature, you know, and that would be something good for ApartmentIQ, too.
If… if it's something you guys can look into.

But it's just an SSO setting that basically… I have to get more details from our tech guy on it, but it basically communicates with Entrata, and it looks for that person's email, or whatever it is, however… whatever the identifier is.
And once it looks at it, when they click to log in for that first time, it matches everything up.

So, like, for example, if there is… we put them in as a leasing person, well, and if Entrata, they're a community manager, it will update their access to community manager, and it will give them the access… say we gave them access to ABC123, whereas they need access to ABC123, D, C, you know, it's like different properties, so it updates it automatically, so… I, you know, wanted to put that out there, you know, as a new tool that I've… you know, experience with another application and, you know, see if that's something that maybe you guys may have the possibility for, too, because it will streamline, again, Lauren, our process, and we won't really have to, you know, the only thing we would really have to touch and feel is, you know, the asset managers and things like that.
Sure.
Sure.
It's really funny you bring that up, Seneca.
I think in the last two weeks… was this, like, a recent update, perhaps, on their end?
Because in the last two weeks, this is maybe the third or fourth time I've heard, and it's not something that we currently support.
But I think it sounds like we're gonna start hearing that, and we're gonna have to prioritize that kind of an update.
We have SSO functionality across a variety of identity providers.
Yes.

And we have a light, or a very basic version of what is referred to as SCIM, and what SCIM effectively allows you to do is to pull the assignments and automatically provision from the identity provider.
right into ApartmentIQ, exactly as you're describing.
Okay.
We don't have the version that would… that would hear and listen to those assignments and automatically make them in IQ.
That's not the iteration we work with right now.
Okay, okay.
But it's… that's the next step, and that would be… what allows this to work out in the way you're describing, so I'm definitely going to put that forward in our product meeting.
With a name like Mill Creek asking for it, in conjunction with a few other partners, I'm sure this is something we'll have on our radar, soon.
To make life easier.
Okay, cool.
And I will definitely talk to our, our tech person is, is, Brooks.
So, just to kind of make you aware, Lauren, that I'm who I'm speaking of, I'm talking about Brooks.
I'll check with Brooks.
Excuse me.
And, see, you know, if we can look at something on our end, and see if it's, you know, what it is.
And I can get that back to you guys in our next meeting.
Perfect.
Perfect.
That sounds great.
Okay.
What a productive meeting, this feels great, Lauren.
I know, I know, I'm really.
Great invite.
So, yeah.
Wonderful to meet you, Seneca.
Nice to meet you as well.
us continue to improve moving forward.
So, you know, it's like, as we start bringing in more faces, I think it's important to have them on this call, so…
Absolutely.
Yeah, perfect.
Excellent.
Well, thank you guys for inviting me and having me.
Of course.
That's hard.
Great ideas.
So, yeah, love it.
All right, Lauren, you know where to find me if there's anything else.
Seneca, have a great day, great meeting you, and we'll talk soon.
Alrighty, take care.
Perfect.
Take care now.
Bye.

Bye.
